data.title: NBCC Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
NBCC (India) Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 29,101.98 million compared to INR 24,457.26 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 30,171.57 million compared to INR 25,129.59 million a year ago. Net income was INR 1,535.16 million compared to INR 1,221.17 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.57 compared to INR 0.45 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.57 compared to INR 0.45 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 53,013.84 million compared to INR 45,882.61 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 54,826.47 million compared to INR 47,091.68 million a year ago. Net income was INR 2,856.46 million compared to INR 2,267.38 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 1.06 compared to INR 0.84 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 1.06 compared to INR 0.84 a year ago.

Wilson talks about how the lack of economic data could impact the Federal Reserve's ability to cut rates, but he says some data has been erratic and less reliable. He speaks on Bloomberg Radio.

Fabric8Labs, Inc. announced that it has received $50 million in funding from a group of investors
data.description: Fabric8Labs, Inc. announced that it has received $50 million in a round of funding led by returning investors New Enterprise Associates, Inc. and Intel Capital Corporation on November 13, 2025. The company has issued Convertible Preferred Stock in the transaction. The transaction included participation from new investors SK hynix Inc., Marunouchi Innovation Partners Co., Ltd., Ericsson Ventures, Masco Ventures, a fund managed by, Masco Corporation, Investment Arm, Toppan Global Venture Partners, Inc. and returning investors Lam Research Capital, TDK Ventures, Inc. and Schneider Electric Ventures.

data.title: Los Angeles Community College District to Offer $300 Million in Bonds for Repairs, Modernization
data.description:
By Patrick Sheridan


The Los Angeles Community College District plans to sell $300 million in bonds for the modernization and equipping of classroom facilities.

The tax-exempt Series F 2016 Election General Obligation Bonds have maturities ranging from 2026 to 2031. Pricing is scheduled for Nov. 20 with closing slated for Dec. 16, as per the roadshow document accompanying the preliminary official statement posted Wednesday on MuniOS.

Interest on the bonds is payable semiannually on Feb. 1 and Aug. 1 of each year, commencing Feb. 1, 2026.

The bonds are general obligation bonds approved by voters within the district and are payable solely from ad valorem property taxes levied annually during the period the bonds are outstanding.

The bond measure called for upgrading security and technology at the district's colleges, as well as repairing or replacing outdated classrooms, science labs and critical infrastructure. Projects include the removal of lead paint and asbestos, providing handicapped accessibility, improving earthquake safety and replacing outdated electrical wiring, according to the Build LACCD website.

The Los Angeles Community College District encompasses an area covering more than 882 square miles including the City of Los Angeles and 39 neighboring cities and communities. The district serves approximately 216,862 students annually at nine accredited colleges, according to the roadshow document.

S&P Global Ratings and Moody's Ratings have issued AA+ and Aaa ratings on the bonds, respectively.

Joint senior managers on the offering are BofA Securities and Ramirez & Co.


Write to Patrick Sheridan at patrick.sheridan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1307ET

Monte dei Paschi Successfully Places EUR500 Million Green Bond
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena Spa announced on Thursday that it has successfully completed the placement of its first green senior preferred unsecured bond, raising a total of EUR500 million.

The bond, which carries an annual fixed interest rate of 3.25%, has a maturity of six years and three months, with a scheduled redemption in February 2032 and an early call option after five years.

The offering attracted demand of approximately EUR2 billion--four times the available amount--demonstrating strong interest from both Italian and international investors. The re-offer price was set at 99.647%, with a spread of 90 basis points above the mid-swap, 30 basis points lower than the initial guidance and 40 basis points below the most recent similar issue in May.

Geographically, orders were distributed with Italy accounting for 36%, followed by Germany, Austria, and Switzerland at 17%, France at 15%, Southern Europe at 13%, and other countries at 19%. A significant share also came from investors with ESG-focused strategies.

The issuance forms part of MPS's 2025 Funding Plan and its 2024 Green, Social and Sustainability Bond Framework, with proceeds earmarked for sustainable environmental initiatives.

The bond will be listed on the Luxembourg Stock Exchange, with expected ratings of 'Baa3' from Moody's, 'BBB-' from Fitch, and 'BBB' from Morningstar DBRS. The placement was managed by BBVA, Deutsche Bank, HSBC, JPMorgan, Mediobanca, Natixis, and NatWest.

MPS shares closed Thursday up 2.0% at EUR8.79 each.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions: redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Tesla Looks to Add Support for Apple CarPlay, Bloomberg Says
data.description:
-- Tesla is developing support for Apple's CarPlay car interaction system in its vehicles, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

-- Tesla has started testing the capability internally, the people said.

-- Tesla has discussed offering CarPlay in the coming months, but a plan hasn't been finalized and a release could come later, according to the report.

-- Tesla didn't respond to requests for comment and a representative for Apple declined to comment, Bloomberg said.

-- CarPlay shows users a version of the iPhone's software on a car's built-in display, with Apple apps for messages, music and maps as well as some third-party apps.


Full article at https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-11-13/tesla-is-working-to-add-apple-carplay-in-bid-to-boost-vehicle-sales?srnd=homepage-americas


Write to kelly.cloonan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1306ET

Code-gen startup Cursor nearly triples its valuation in Coatue-led funding round
data.description:
(Reuters) -Code-generation startup Cursor nearly tripled its valuation to $29.3 billion in five months after raising $2.3 billion in its latest funding round, as artificial intelligence companies continue to attract investor attention.

The Series D funding round was led by new investor Coatue, an investment management firm, and existing investor Accel, Cursor said in a blog post on Thursday. Fresh investors Nvidia and Alphabet's Google also participated in the round.

AI firms have dominated private funding markets this year, with global venture funding in the third quarter increasing 38% year-over-year to $97 billion, about half of which went to AI companies, according to data from Crunchbase.

"(The round) is a resounding current affirmation of AI prospects...but the commitments for this raise most likely accumulated over the last several weeks or months and are very specific to Cursor's prospects," said Michael Ashley Schulman, chief investment officer at Running Point Capital Advisors.

A surge in investor appetite for AI-linked firms also helped drive Wall Street's benchmark indexes to record highs this year.

The San Francisco-based company raised $900 million in June at a $9.9 billion valuation, attracting backing from investors, including Thrive Capital, Andreessen Horowitz and Accel.

The company has crossed $1 billion in annualized revenue, with sales-led revenue increasing 100-fold since the beginning of 2025, Cursor said in a mailed statement to Reuters.

Cursor, which develops tools to autonomously generate and complete code, said the latest funding round will be used to invest in its research efforts.

Code-generation startups are attracting sky-high valuations as businesses explore artificial intelligence-based solutions to enhance or replace traditional software development roles.

However, investor concerns that valuations of AI companies may have outpaced fundamentals intensified after SoftBank Group offloaded its $5.8 billion stake in Nvidia earlier in the week.

(Reporting by Pritam Biswas in Bengaluru; Editing by Vijay Kishore)

By Pritam Biswas

data.title: Novo Nordisk Raises $4.7 Billion in Bond Sale to Fund Akero Acquisition
U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise as Production Hits Record
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup


U.S. crude oil inventories increased more than expected last week as U.S. production hit a record high and exports fell, according to data released Thursday by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Commercial crude oil stocks excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve rose by 6.4 million barrels, to 427.6 million barrels, in the week ended Nov. 7, and were about 4% below the five-year average for the time of year, the EIA said. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey had expected a 400,000 barrel crude stock build.

U.S. crude oil production rose by 211,000 barrels a day, to just under 13.9 million barrels a day, the highest on record, according to EIA estimates. Crude imports were down by 703,000 barrels a day, at 5.2 million barrels a day, while exports fell by 1.6 million barrels a day, to 2.8 million barrels a day.

Oil held in the SPR increased by 798,000 barrels, to 410.4 million barrels. Stocks at Cushing, Okla., the Nymex delivery hub, were down by 346,000 barrels, at 22.5 million barrels.

Refinery capacity use rose by 3.4 percentage points, to 89.4%, against expectations in the Journal survey for a 0.6 perentage-point increase. Crude input to refineries was up by 717,000 barrels a day, at 16 million barrels a day.

Gasoline inventories fell by 945,000 barrels, to 205.1 million barrels, and were 4% below the five-year average, the EIA said. Gasoline demand averaged 9 million barrels a day, up by 145,000 barrels a day from the week before. Gasoline stocks were seen falling by 2 million barrels.

Distillate fuel stocks fell by 637,000 barrels, to 110.9 million barrels, and were 8% below the five-year average. Distillate inventories were forecast to have declined by 1.5 million barrels. 


 
Change in U.S. oil inventories for the week ended Nov. 7: 
 
                   Crude       Gasoline      Distillates        Refinery Use 
EIA data:           6.4          -0.9           -0.6                 3.4 
Forecast:           0.4          -2.0           -1.5                 0.6

Note: Numbers in millions of barrels, with the exception of refinery use, which is in percentage points.


Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1249ET

NYMEX Overview : Oil Rebounds After Wednesday's Selloff -- OPIS
data.description:
Crude oil contracts were trading higher around midday Thursday as the petroleum market regained its footing after the previous session's steep losses driven by renewed oversupply fears.

At 12:05 p.m. ET, the more-active January NYMEX West Texas Intermediate crude futures were up by 30cts to about $58.80/bbl, and December WTI was 35cts higher to $58.85/bbl.

London-based January ICE Brent prices were up by 40cts to around $63.15/bbl and February Brent was 35cts higher to $62.80/bbl.

Both WTI and Brent finished almost $3 lower on Wednesday after OPEC said in its latest monthly forecast that global supply could catch up to demand by 2026.

Earlier Thursday, the Paris-based International Energy Agency in its monthly report raised its forecasts for global oil supply and demand for this year and 2026, which would result in even larger surpluses for both years.

Refined product futures were mixed. January NYMEX RBOB was up by 2.1cts to $1.909/gal and December RBOB was 2.6cts higher to $1.9815/gal. January ULSD, however, was down 1.3cts to $2.4245/gal and December ULSD was 1.4cts lower to $2.468/gal.

Petroleum futures did not show dramatic changes immediately following the release of the weekly data by the Energy Information Administration. The latest EIA data showed U.S. commercial crude oil stocks increased 6.4 million bbl for the week ended Friday, while gasoline and distillate inventory were both down by less than 1 million bbl.

In the spot market, U.S. Gulf Coast CBOB gasoline rose nearly 5cts ahead of a scheduling deadline later Thursday. Other cash products mostly mirrored changes in NYMEX futures.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 


   --Reporting by Frank Tang, ftang@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly,   mkelly@opisnet.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1246ET

Pfizer Completes Acquisition of Metsera
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan


Pfizer closed its acquisition of weight-loss drug startup Metsera in a deal that could be valued at more than $10 billion.

Under the deal, Pfizer paid $65.60 a share in cash up front, and could additionally pay a contingent value right of up to $20.65 a share tied to specific clinical and regulatory milestones, the pharmaceutical company said Thursday.

Metsera is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer, and its shares will stop trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market after market close, Pfizer said.

The deal marks an investment in the future of medicine, according to Pfizer.

"By acquiring Metsera, we are directing our resources toward one of the most impactful and high-growth therapeutic areas and positioning ourselves to define it," Pfizer Chief Executive Albert Bourla said.

The deal follows a heated bidding war with rival Novo Nordisk. In September, Pfizer had agreed to buy Metsera for up to $7.3 billion, which Novo Nordisk topped with an unsolicited bid valued at up to $9 billion.

After Pfizer reached its deal with Metsera last week, Novo Nordisk said it wouldn't increase its offer because of its commitment to financial discipline and shareholder value. The company said it will keep assessing opportunities for business development and acquisition.

Metsera, meanwhile, said it decided to take Pfizer's offer in part because of a call from the Federal Trade Commission warning about potential risks to proceeding with a Novo Nordisk deal.


Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1239ET
